rent.note: furnished 1BR; ARS 18,000–35,000/mo = ~$270–$525 USD; smaller city keeps rents lower

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; peso-denominated = very cheap for USD holders

groceries.note: home cooking; excellent locro and empanada culture; local markets cheap

diningOut.note: local parrillas and regional cuisine; very affordable in USD

transit.note: buses + taxis; city center compact and walkable

coworking.note: limited formal coworking; cafes fill the gap

simData.note: Personal/Claro 30-day; internet 86 Mbps avg per Novad data

misc.note: gym, toiletries, entertainment, Andes/Quebrada day trips, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Salta tourist season peaks Jul–Sep (dry, cool Andean winter).

evidence.claim: Salta monthly cost ~$967–$1,236; internet 86 Mbps avg
evidence.quote: Salta has a monthly cost of living around $1,236 with average internet speeds of 86 Mbps
A one-bedroom apartment can cost anywhere from ARS 18,000 to 35,000 (USD 270 - 525) per month, depending on the neighborhood and amenities

evidence.claim: smaller cities like Salta 20–40% cheaper than Buenos Aires
evidence.quote: Smaller cities like Córdoba or Salta are even more budget-friendly compared to Buenos Aires; smaller cities in Argentina are 20–40% cheaper than Buenos Aires

evidence.claim: authentic charm and low cost; not most polished for nomads but improving
evidence.quote: Salta may not be the most polished destination for digital nomads, but its authentic charm and low cost of living make it an attractive option for those looking to experience the real Argentina
